original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults, children, and young people with established ulcerative colitis.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Supports symptom, disease-course, treatment, monitoring, growth, bone-health, patient-view, and clinician-entered follow-up documentation without assigning severity or generating management.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.3 — information, treatment context, and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting current symptoms, previous response, adverse effects, patient preferences, and clinician review context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.8 — monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed monitoring results, growth, bone-health context, and communication across services.

title: Ulcerative colitis: management — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
population: Adults, children, and young people with established ulcerative colitis.
intended_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
